title: Implement WebSocketClient subscribe mode
markdown:
## Goal

Extend `WebSocketClient` to support subscriptions. Clients can subscribe to a URI prefix and receive push notifications via a callback, replacing polling for real-time use cases.

## Background

G1-A5-01 added subscription support to the server side. The `WebSocketClient` currently only handles request/response messages. It needs to:

1. Send `subscribe` / `unsubscribe` requests
2. Handle server-initiated `notification` messages (which have no matching request ID)
3. Route notifications to registered callbacks

The message handler currently ignores any message without a matching pending request ID. Notifications need a separate dispatch path.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `WebSocketClient` has a `subscribe(prefix: string, callback: SubscriptionCallback): Promise<Subscription>` method
- [ ] `SubscriptionCallback` type: `(uri: string, data: unknown, version?: number) => void`
- [ ] `Subscription` type: `{ id: string; unsubscribe(): Promise<void> }`
- [ ] Server-initiated notifications (messages without request IDs) are routed to matching subscription callbacks
- [ ] `unsubscribe()` sends unsubscribe request to server and removes local callback
- [ ] Multiple subscriptions on the same client work independently
- [ ] Subscriptions survive reconnection (re-subscribed after reconnect)
- [ ] `cleanup()` unsubscribes all active subscriptions
- [ ] Tests: subscribe, receive notification, unsubscribe, reconnect re-subscribe

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-client-ws/mod.ts` to understand current message handling
2. Add subscription tracking: `Map<string, { prefix: string, callback: SubscriptionCallback }>` (subscriptionId -> subscription)
3. Modify `handleMessage`:
   - If message has `type: "notification"`, find matching subscriptions by prefix and invoke callbacks
   - Otherwise, handle as existing request/response
4. Add `subscribe(prefix, callback)`:
   - Send `{ type: "subscribe", payload: { prefix } }` request
   - On success, store subscription locally with returned ID
   - Return `Subscription` object with `unsubscribe()` method
5. Add reconnection logic:
   - After reconnection, iterate all active subscriptions and re-send subscribe requests
   - This goes in the `connect()` success handler
6. Add tests in `libs/b3nd-client-ws/mod_test.ts` (may need mock WebSocket)

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-client-ws/mod.ts` -- add subscribe/unsubscribe methods, notification handling, reconnect re-subscription
- `libs/b3nd-client-ws/mod_test.ts` -- tests for subscription lifecycle

## Testing

```bash
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-client-ws/
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A5-01 (server-side subscription protocol)
- **Blocks**: G1-A5-03 (React hooks need client subscribe API)
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Design WebSocket subscription protocol (server-push for URI prefix changes)
- [ ] Implement server-side: node tracks subscriptions, pushes matching messages
- [ ] Implement `WebSocketClient` subscribe mode (beyond current request/response)
- [ ] Add React hooks for subscriptions (`useSubscription(prefix)`)
